metadata.title:
WOMAN'S CLUB, UNION LABOR
AND GERMAN SOCIETIES


MEET

A STRONG RESOLUTION

MEMBERS SHOULD BE GIVEN


POLICE POWERS

pages.raw_text:
TT either Mayor Pratt, the coun.


Gil or Acting Chief OF Polfc0


Sullivan has the idea that the
good government forces IN this
town are going to have any white-
wash investigation OF the city Jail
conditions, then those gentlemen
want to back up and guess again.
Yesterday afternoon the leaders
Of the. Woman's club, union labor
and the United German societies
met in conference and after digest
ing Mayor Pratt's refusal to In
crease the investigating committee
to five members, ana hts announce
ment that he wanted a public I'm,
vestigation," the leaders decided
that unless there was some slight
semblance of real probing to this
inquiry they would have none of It.
and so Mayor Pratt is requested to
increase the committee to five, add
ing one member each from the Ger.
man societies and union labor, and
to have these inquiry sessions se-
cret. and finally to give the COM
mittee special police powers SO that
members of the department need
not be called In to help investigate
themselves.


And unless the mayor sees lit to
SO make this investigating body a
real live force, there will be some
real, sure enough, private investi-
eating done If 1t requires a special
grand jury and new public prose,
cutor to get results.


Any fond hope that the various
official schemers may have had
about this campaign being speedily
choked. and any delusion that the
various cogs in the machine may
have entertained about being able
to bamfoozle the various leaders
with gush about tried and true pO-
lice officials who deserved a public
investigation" will likely be-
brushed away by the following,
adopted by the three above named
organizations yesterday afternoon:


To His Honor. Mayor Pratt. Dear
Sir Noting your determination to
limit the members Of the Jail In
vestigatiOn committee to three
members and your refusal to in
crease this committee to five, add
ing one Of the United German So
cieties and one from the labor or
ganizations. we, the undersigned,
representing the Woman's club, the
United German societies and or
ganized labor, do request that this
investigating committee be in
creased by the addition of mem,
ber from the ranks Of union labor
and a member from the United
German societies.


Further noting your desire for q
I, public investigation into jail con
ditions, we. the undersigned of the
three above named organizations,
do urge that this policy be not fol-
lowed In justice to the witnesses
on whom the success OF the investi-
CATION must depend. and request
for the sake of the public good that
this committee pursue Its inquiry In
secret and that every means be-


given II to discover the real truth
of Jail and police conditions.
| To really make the committee
available for active investigation
we believe that each member
should be given full police power
as special officer. so that no
member Of the regular force need
be relied on to aid In the inquiry of
his own department.


MRS. A. IL. HOUSE


u. G. COATES


KARL RIEDELSBEROER
